hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (368) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,471, a 25% ratio - the weakest of the entire raid night), and overheal (76.9%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (60.4%) by 16.5 points - the largest overheal gap of the raid night - landing at the 13th percentile, the lowest of the raid night, though with zero real deaths logged.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 36 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap, and the first of a consistent pattern across this entire raid night.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 85%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap this kill. Every other tracked cooldown matched the sample. No deaths to answer.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (91.3% vs. 77.5% average, +13.8) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (51.6% vs. 47.7% average) tracking close to typical, across 9 distinct targets.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (771) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,759, a 44% ratio), and overheal (43.2%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (41.6%) by a real but narrow 1.6 points - active time (76.1% vs. 93.1% average, a real 17-point gap) stands out as the more notable issue here - a middling 53rd-percentile kill with zero real deaths.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 74.8 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the largest composition gaps of the raid night.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield went uncast against a 72%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap this kill. Every other tracked cooldown matched the sample.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (46.3% vs. 52.1% average) and top-target concentration (11.6% vs. 16.8% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (772) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,971), with 22 real deaths logged - by far the most of any kill this raid night, and among the highest death counts seen across this whole project - yet this still landed at a middling 46th percentile, with overheal (39.7%) actually running below the sample's median.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 65.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Only two real cooldowns were cast the entire kill (one Mana Tide Totem, one Mana Potion), so they register as the 'nearest' cooldown to nearly all 22 deaths purely by default - not real saves for most of them. The closest real timing (Banepain, 330 milliseconds before the Mana Potion cast) is coincidental - Mana Potion is self-only and can't answer another player's death. Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 87%-used norm - a real gap, though not the headline issue on a kill this chaotic.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (47.2% vs. 51.2% average) and top-target concentration (12% vs. 14% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 25 distinct targets - the widest spread of the raid night, consistent with a mass-death, chaotic fight.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,127) ran below the Top 100 average (2,159), but overheal (22.7%) ran a real 21.8 points better than the sample's single worst parse (44.5%) - the cleanest efficiency number of the raid night - landing at the 67th percentile, the best of the raid, with 2 real deaths logged.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 29.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the smaller composition gaps of the raid night.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 87%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. Neither of the 2 real deaths (Ghostpepperx, Megatauro) lands close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save - the nearest timings are an Earth Shield cast on a different target and a self-cast Mana Potion.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (67.8% vs. 74% average) and top-target concentration (32.5% vs. 34.5% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 21 distinct targets.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (788) ran below the Top 100 average (1,532), and overheal (50.1%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (53.8%, a 3.7-point gap) - active time (99%) was the strongest of the raid night, above even the Top 100 average - a middling 50th-percentile kill, with 2 real deaths logged (the same player, Lasmur, twice).
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 74.9 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the largest composition gap of the entire raid night.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample - no real deviation. Both of Lasmur's deaths land nearest the same self-cast Mana Potion (11 and 26 seconds out) - coincidental timing, not a save.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (54.9% vs. 51.9% average) and top-target concentration (21.4% vs. 22.1% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 24 distinct targets - the second-widest spread of the raid night.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (625) ran below the Top 100 average (1,085), and overheal (44.6%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (41.2%) by 3.4 points - active time (83.7% vs. 94.2% average, a real 10.5-point gap) was also a real issue - on the raid's longest kill this night (482 seconds), with 2 real deaths logged, including Vajomee's own.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 77.2 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the largest composition gap of the entire raid night.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield was cast 5 times (Thugalina x2, Braila x2, Kilsby) - the heaviest use of the raid night, in line with the sample. Both Ancestral Swiftness (95%-used norm) and Dark Rune (50%-used norm, the highest baseline usage of Dark Rune on any boss this raid night) went completely uncast - real gaps. Neither Ghostpepperx's death nor Vajomee's own lands close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save - the nearest logged cast (Earth Shield, on a different target) is 39-70 seconds removed from each.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44.7% vs. 46.8% average) and top-target concentration (12.7% vs. 17.5% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (395) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,121), and overheal (52.6%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (50.6%) by 2 points - active time (78% vs. 96.2% average, a real 18.2-point gap) was also notable - landing at the 27th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 1 real death logged. This is also the one kill this raid night with no Battle or Guardian Elixir active at all (food only) - a real, confirmed consumable gap that may plausibly explain part of the weak HPM and active-time numbers here specifically.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 54.8 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample - 5 real Earth Shields (Braila x4, Captinhelp), tied for the heaviest count of the raid night. Captinhelp's death doesn't land close enough to any cooldown (the nearest, Earth Shield, is nearly 2 minutes removed) to call a real save.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (60.2% vs. 54.5% average) and top-target concentration (17.7% vs. 23.6% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,497) ran below the Top 100 average (2,650), and overheal (28.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (25%) by 3.4 points - a middling 49th-percentile kill, with zero real deaths logged.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 66.1 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the larger composition gaps of the raid night.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 89%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. Earth Shield's own real usage rate here is a much lower 37%, so its zero casts isn't itself a deviation.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (48.1% vs. 42.7% average) and top-target concentration (11.4% vs. 11% average) both tracked almost exactly on the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (517) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,654), and overheal (59.7%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (56.7%) by 3 points - landing at the 22nd percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 9 real deaths logged, the second-most of the raid night, including Vajomee's own.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran a real but comparatively modest 10.6 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the smallest composition gap of the entire raid night.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. All 9 real deaths (including Vajomee's own, just 715 milliseconds after the sole Ancestral Swiftness cast) land within about 12 seconds of each other and of that one cast - this reads as a single burst or raid-wide event rather than a sequence of individual healing failures, and the timing is too tight for that one cast to have meaningfully answered any of them.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (46.4% vs. 51.3% average) and top-target concentration (10.3% vs. 15.3% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (595) ran below the Top 100 average (1,029), but active time (64.6% vs. 86.6% average, a real 22-point gap) is the standout number here - the lowest active time of the entire raid night, on the raid's longest kill (526 seconds) - with 4 real deaths logged, including Vajomee's own mid-fight, which plausibly explains much of the reduced activity (once dead, there's no more healing to log for the rest of the encounter). This still landed at a solid 59th percentile, the second-best of the raid night.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 44.5 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield was cast 6 times (Braila, Thugalina x4, Captinhelp) - the heaviest use of the entire raid night, in line with the sample. Vinnyvozz's death and Vajomee's own both land within seconds to low double-digit seconds of an Earth Shield cast, but neither was actually targeted at them (the casts went to Braila/Thugalina/Captinhelp) - coincidental proximity, not a confirmed save.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (49.5% vs. 48.5% average) and top-target concentration (22.1% vs. 17.9% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 26 distinct targets - the widest spread of the entire raid night.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: consistent, aside from one likely-benign single-kill gear variance (a missing temporary weapon enchant on Kael'thas only)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Two real gear gaps stand out: the Feet slot (item 28752) and the Back slot (item 28765) both carry no permanent enchant across every kill this raid night - two cheap, static enchants that would apply immediately and don't require any fight-specific loadout choice.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 10 real bosses attempted were killed this raid night, though Hydross required a real wipe before the successful kill shown here. This was a genuinely rough night by the numbers: raw HPS trailed the Top 100 average on every single kill (25%-58% of the benchmark average), and Vajomee never ranked 1st by raw healing on any of the 10 kills against the other tracked healers present, though item-level rank did reach 1st on 3 of 10 (44% average rank percentile). Chain Heal's composition share ran ahead of the Top 100 average on every single kill, from a modest 10.6 points (Solarian) to a full 77.2 points (Vashj) - the most consistent single pattern of the raid night. Active time ran a real 10-22 points below the Top 100 average on 5 of 10 kills (Lurker, Vashj, Alar, Solarian, and especially Kael'thas at 64.6%, the lowest of the raid) - Vajomee's own mid-fight deaths on Vashj, Solarian, and Kael'thas plausibly explain a real share of this. Deaths were heavily concentrated on Leotheras (22, by far the most of the raid night and among the highest seen across this whole project) and Solarian (9, nearly all within a single 12-second burst window including Vajomee's own) - Hydross, Lurker, and Void Reaver were the only three clean kills. Alar was the one kill with no Battle or Guardian Elixir active at all, food only - a real, confirmed consumable gap.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Vajomee never ranked 1st by raw healing on any of the 10 kills this raid night, and active time ran a real 10-22 points below the Top 100 average on half the kills. Combined with Leotheras's 22 real deaths and three separate mid-fight deaths for Vajomee (Vashj, Solarian, Kael'thas), this was the roughest raid night on record for this healer - see each boss's own page for the real numbers.

GearCheckItems.Description: MainHand's temporary enchant is missing on a single kill
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: The weapon itself and its permanent enchant are unchanged all raid night - only the temporary (weapon oil/stone) enchant is absent on Kael'thas, consistent with it simply running out and not being reapplied before that pull rather than a real gearing gap.

GearCheckItems.Description: No Battle or Guardian Elixir active on Al'ar
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Only the Well Fed food buff was active on this kill - a real, confirmed gap in the consumable setup, the only incomplete kill of the 10 this raid night.
